rules.description: File names must use PascalCase and end with .cs.
Partial class files must use dot-notation: StudentService.Validations.cs.
Partial class files must NOT use concatenation: StudentServiceValidations.cs is forbidden.
Partial class files must NOT use underscore: StudentService_Validations.cs is forbidden.
Variable names must be full and descriptive. Single letters and abbreviations are forbidden.
Lambda parameter names must be full: students.Where(student => ...) not students.Where(s => ...).
Plural collections use natural plural: students not studentList, studentCollection.
No type suffix in variable names: student not studentModel, studentObj, studentEntity.
Variables with null/default values must signal intent: Student noStudent = null not Student student = null.
Zero-value numeric variables must signal intent: int noChangeCount = 0 not int changeCount = 0.
When right-side type is clear (new Student()), use var.
When right-side type is NOT clear (method call), declare with explicit type.
When right-side type is anonymous, var is required.
Single-property: assign after construction. Multi-property: use initializer.
Variable declaration exceeding 120 characters must break after the = sign.
Multi-line declarations must have a blank line before AND after them.
Single-line declarations must have NO blank lines between consecutive single-line declarations.
Method names must contain a verb.
Async methods must end with Async suffix.
Input parameter names must be fully qualified: studentName not name, text, value.
Action parameters must identify the property acted on: GetStudentByIdAsync(Guid studentId).
When passing literals or non-matching variable names, the parameter alias is required.
Methods with exactly one line must use fat arrow syntax.
Methods with multiple lines must use a scope body {}.
Single-liner exceeding 120 chars must break after => with one extra tab indentation.
Multi-liner methods with chaining must use a scope body (not fat arrow).
Multi-line methods must have a blank line before the return statement.
Consecutive calls under 120 chars may stack without blank lines (unless final is return).
Calls exceeding 120 chars require blank line separation from adjacent calls.
Method declarations exceeding 120 characters must break parameters onto the next line.
When parameters are broken onto new lines, each parameter must be on its own line.
Method chaining: first call on same line as subject, each subsequent call indented one extra tab.
Model class names carry no type suffix: Student not StudentModel, StudentDTO.
Service class names are singular PascalCase + Service: StudentService not StudentsService.
Broker class names are singular PascalCase + Broker: StudentBroker not StudentsBroker.
Controller class names are plural PascalCase + Controller: StudentsController not StudentController.
Class fields are named in camelCase: studentName not StudentName, _studentName.
Class fields follow the same naming rules as variables (descriptive, no abbreviation).
Private class fields must be referenced using this.: this.studentName = studentName.
When passing literals, named aliases are required: new Student(name: 'Josh') not new Student('Josh').
When variable names match constructor aliases, aliases are not required.
Target-typed new() is forbidden: Student student = new (...) is not allowed.
Instantiation property assignment order must match the class declaration property order.
Copyright block must use the exact Standard format with // ------ dash lines.
XML-style copyright comments (<copyright>) are forbidden.
Block comment copyright (/* ... */) is forbidden.
Methods inaccessible at dev-time should document: Purpose, Incomes, Outcomes, Side Effects.
